GEN: A&O, NAD, comfortable.
HEENT: Sclerae non-icteric, PEARLA, EOM intact, o/p clear, MMM.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no thyromegaly.
Lymph nodes: No cervical or supraclavicular LAD.
CV: S1S2, RRR, no MRG.
RESP: Good air movement bilaterally, no rhonchi or wheezing.
BACK: No spine, CVA, or iliac tenderness.
ABD: Soft, non-tender, non distended.
EXTR: LLE +1 edema, no RLE edema, no calf tenderness, no finger clubbing.
DERM: No rash.
Neuro: Strength 5/5, non-focal.
PSYCH: Appropriate and calm.
TTE [**2154-3-22**]: There is mild symmetric left ventricular hypertrophy
The left ventricular cavity size is normal. Overall left ventricular systolic function is normal (LVEF 60%). However, the posterior wall and apex appear hypokinetic.
Right ventricular chamber size and free wall motion are normal. There are three aortic valve leaflets. The aortic valve leaflets are moderately thickened.
There is moderate aortic valve stenosis (valve area 1.0-1.2cm2). Mild to moderate ([**1-21**]+) aortic regurgitation is seen.
The mitral valve leaflets are mildly thickened.
There is no mitral valve prolapse. Mild (1+) mitral regurgitation is seen. [Due to acoustic shadowing, the severity of mitral regurgitation may be significantly UNDERestimated.]
The tricuspid valve leaflets are mildly thickened.
There is mild pulmonary artery systolic hypertension. There is no pericardial effusion.
Compared with the findings of the prior study (images reviewed) of [**2154-2-27**], the findings are similar, but the technically suboptimal nature of both studies precludes definitive comparison.
EKG: New ST depressions in V2-V5 with ST elevation present in aVR
Cardiac catheterization: 1. Coronary angiography of this right dominant system demonstrated left main and three vessel coronary artery disease
The LMCA had a 90% ostial and 70% distal lesion. The LAD is totally occluded after D1 with RCA collaterals. The LCx has a 90% mid-vessel stenosis.
The RCA has a focal 80% stenosis in one branch of the dual PDA system.
2. Limited resting hemodynamics reveal systemic arterial hypertension with central aortic pressure of 162/66 mmHg with mean 99 mmHg.
FINAL DIAGNOSIS: 1. Left main and three vessel coronary artery disease.
2. Not suitable for percutaneous intervention.
3. Discuss possibility of CABG with CT surgery and Oncology.
Left main proximal coronary artery disease
dynamical EKG changes concerning for left main disease.
Cardiology was consulted by Oncology,
referred for cardiac catheterization.
Left heart catheterization [**2154-3-21**] showed 90% left main proximal CAD, 95% LAD with collaterals to the right, 90% mid circ, focal PDA 80%.
Cardiac enzymes remained negative
started on a heparin drip (warfarin was discontinued)
and continued on his home dose of metoprolol, plavix, and simvastatin.
TTE [**2154-3-22**] was similar to TTE done
aspirin allergy
was not administered aspirin given his documented aspirin allergy.
Cardiac surgery was consulted regarding the possibility of CABG, and the decision was made to take the patient for CABG. H
He underwent venous mapping and carotid ultrasound
aspirin allergy
He was continued on oral Plavix given the patient's underlying aspirin allergy and heparin drip
The patient was brought to the operating room on [**2154-4-3**]

underwent Urgent coronary artery bypass graft times 3; left internal mammary artery to left anterior descending artery and saphenous vein grafts to obtuse marginal and diagonal arteries. Endoscopic harvesting of the long saphenous vein.
transferred to the CVICU
in stable condition
transferred to the telemetry floor
Chest tubes and pacing wires were discontinued without complication.
difficult intubation due to limited neck range of motion.
.  He was successfully extubated on POD2
. Aggressive pulmonary toilet, incentive spirometer and nebs his oxygen requirements improved with oxygen saturations
but continues to require IV lasix for ongoing diuresis.
hemodynamically stable sinus rhythm.
low-dose beta-blockers,
bowel function resumed.
Stool softeners and laxative continued.
He was followed by nutrition
and tolerated a regular diet.
chronic renal insufficiency
Diuresis was continued with good urine output
. Electrolytes replete as needed.
Chronic DVT/PE
Warfarin was restarted
Neuro exam non-focal.  Pain well controlled.
follow by physical therapy for strengthen and mobility.
